00000.name: Default Functionalities
00000.description: This demo for Essential JS 2 PdfViewer shows the default rendering of the PdfViewer with minimum configuration.

00001.name: Smart Fill
00001.description: This demo showcases the PDF Viewer AI feature.

00002.name: Smart Redact
00002.description: This demo showcases the PDF Viewer AI feature.

00003.name: Summarizer
00003.description: This demo showcases the PDF Viewer AI feature.

00004.name: Read-Only
00004.description: This sample demonstrates the Pdf Viewer in read only mode

00005.name: Document List
00005.description: This sample demonstrates to open PDF document in Read-only mode and Editable mode using PDF Viewer in Pop-up dialog

00006.name: Multi-Format Viewer
00006.description: This sample demonstrates to loads various file types like PDFs, images, and Microsoft Office documents (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) into the PDF Viewer

00007.name: Organize Pages
00007.description: This sample demonstrates the page organization features of the PDF Viewer component, allowing users to effortlessly insert, delete, rearrange, copy, undo, redo, and rotate pages.

00008.name: Redaction
00008.description: This PDF viewer sample facilitates the permanent removal of sensitive or confidential data from PDF files.

00009.name: Toolbar
00009.description: This demo for Essential JS 2 PdfViewer shows the default rendering of the PdfViewer with custom toolbar configuration.

000010.name: Context Menu
000010.description: This demo for Essential JS 2 PdfViewer shows the custom options for users who use to add custom options in context menu.

000011.name: Right to Left
000011.description: This demo for Essential JS 2 PdfViewer shows the default rendering of the PdfViewer with Right to Left Configuration.

000012.name: Form Filling
000012.description: This demo for Essential JS 2 PdfViewer shows the default rendering of the PdfViewer with form filling configuration.

000013.name: Form Designer
000013.description: This sample demonstrates the creation of the supported Form fields in the PDF Viewer such as Textbox, Password, Checkbox, Radio Button, Drop Down, List box, Signature, and Initial. We can also customize these fields and can include new fields through the user interaction by switching to the designer mode.

000014.name: eSigning Form Designer
000014.description: This sample demonstrates the creation of the supported Form fields in the PDF Viewer such as Textbox, Password, Checkbox, Radio Button, Drop Down, List box, Signature, and Initial. We can also customize these fields and can include new fields through the user interaction by switching to the designer mode.

000015.name: eSigning PDF Forms
000015.description: This sample demonstrates the creation of the supported Form fields in the PDF Viewer such as Textbox, Password, Checkbox, Radio Button, Drop Down, List box, Signature, and Initial. We can also customize these fields and can include new fields through the user interaction by switching to the designer mode.

000016.name: Annotations
000016.description: This sample demonstrates the creation of different types of annotations such as Text Markup, Shapes, Measurements, Free text, Stamps, Handwritten signature, ink, and sticky notes in the PDF Viewer. And also we can customize the added annotations or we can include new annotations through user interaction from the PDF Viewer.

000017.name: Programmatic Operations
000017.description: This sample showcases how to programmatically create or edit various types of annotations in the SfPdfViewer. These annotations include Text Markup, Shapes, Measurements, Free text, Stamps, Ink, and Sticky notes. Additionally, we can programmatically insert new annotations or programmatically update existing ones.

000018.name: Handwritten Signature
000018.description: This sample demonstrates the Handwritten signature and initial support of the PDF Viewer. The signature or initial support reduces the paperwork of reviewing the contents and it is verified digitally.

000019.name: Invisible Digital Signature
000019.description: This sample demonstrates how to digitally sign a PDF document from code behind using Syncfusion's PDF Viewer and PDF Library

000020.name: Visible Digital Signature
000020.description: This sample demonstrates how to create a visible sign in a PDF document digitally from code behind using Syncfusion's PDF Viewer and PDF Library
